title: blastus eglandulosus
intro: Blastus eglandulosus, also known as the sticky stork's bill, is a plant species native to tropical Africa that belongs to the Stilbaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies. The health benefits of Blastus eglandulosus include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Blastus eglandulosus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its essential oil can also be extracted for topical use and aromatherapy.
